questions.q: Ram Manohar Lohia is best described as a leader of which political tradition?
questions.explanation: Lohia was a prominent socialist leader and thinker.

questions.q: In which legislative body was Ram Manohar Lohia a prominent voice?
questions.explanation: Lohia was a prominent parliamentarian and voice in the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Lohia is especially associated with advocating which approach to governance?
questions.explanation: Lohia strongly advocated decentralisation in governance.

questions.q: Ram Manohar Lohia helped shape which kind of politics in independent India?
questions.explanation: Lohia shaped opposition politics in independent India.

questions.q: Lohia is remembered primarily as a leader, thinker and:
questions.explanation: He is remembered as a socialist leader, thinker and parliamentarian.

questions.q: Lohia's political philosophy centred on which broad idea?
questions.explanation: His political philosophy centred on socialism.

questions.q: Lohia was active in the national movement before independence as part of which broad struggle?
questions.explanation: Lohia was active in the Indian freedom struggle before independence.

questions.q: Lohia's full commonly used name is:
questions.explanation: His full name is Ram Manohar Lohia.

questions.q: Lohia's ideas continue to influence which segment of Indian politics?
questions.explanation: Lohia's ideas continue to influence socialist and several regional parties.

questions.q: Lohia is often grouped with which other figure as a leading socialist of his era?
questions.explanation: Lohia and Jayaprakash Narayan are both regarded as leading socialists of their era.

questions.q: Lohia was a founding figure in which party that emerged from the Congress in the 1930s-40s?
questions.explanation: Lohia was a founding figure in the Congress Socialist Party.

questions.q: Lohia pursued his doctoral studies in which country?
questions.explanation: Lohia earned his doctorate in Germany.

questions.q: Lohia participated in which major 1942 nationwide movement?
questions.explanation: Lohia was active in the Quit India Movement of 1942.

questions.q: Lohia is associated with which political strategy aimed at uniting non-Congress opposition parties?
questions.explanation: Lohia is associated with the strategy of 'non-Congressism' to unite opposition parties.

questions.q: Lohia's social philosophy strongly emphasised the removal of inequalities based on which factors?
questions.explanation: Lohia emphasised removing inequalities of caste and gender, among others.

questions.q: Lohia entered the Lok Sabha after winning a by-election in 1963 from which constituency in Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Lohia entered the Lok Sabha by winning the 1963 Farrukhabad by-election.

questions.q: Lohia is credited with championing the use of which language in administration and public life?
questions.explanation: Lohia campaigned for greater use of Indian languages in place of English in public life.

questions.q: Lohia's economic thinking favoured technology and production suited to which scale?
questions.explanation: Lohia favoured small-scale, decentralised technology and production.

questions.q: Lohia served as a general secretary of which party before later socialist regroupings?
questions.explanation: Lohia held senior organisational roles in the Socialist Party in the post-independence period.

questions.q: Lohia advocated reservation and representation policies to uplift which groups?
options: Backward classes, women and the marginalised
questions.explanation: Lohia advocated greater representation for backward classes, women and marginalised groups.

questions.q: Lohia's well-known idea of organising politics around fundamental issues is sometimes summarised by his emphasis on a few key:
questions.explanation: Lohia articulated a set of guiding principles or planks for socialist politics.

questions.q: In Parliament, Lohia became well known for his pointed critiques delivered through which means?
questions.explanation: Lohia was known for his speeches and debating interventions in Parliament.

questions.q: In which year was Ram Manohar Lohia born?
questions.explanation: Ram Manohar Lohia was born in 1910.

questions.q: In which year did Ram Manohar Lohia die?
questions.explanation: Lohia died in 1967.

questions.q: Lohia was born in a town in which present-day state of India?
questions.explanation: Lohia was born in Akbarpur, in present-day Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: Lohia's framework for analysing the world powers of his time is sometimes summarised as a doctrine opposing both blocs in favour of:
questions.explanation: Lohia advocated a 'third camp' position independent of the major Cold War blocs.

questions.q: Lohia worked in the foreign affairs department of the Congress in the years before independence, dealing chiefly with:
questions.explanation: Lohia worked on international and foreign-policy questions in the Congress before independence.

questions.q: Lohia is associated with the idea of seven kinds of equality or revolutions (the 'Saptakranti'); these were aimed at ending which?
options: Multiple forms of inequality and injustice
questions.explanation: Lohia's concept of seven revolutions aimed at ending several forms of inequality and injustice.

questions.q: Lohia famously raised in Parliament a debate about the daily expenditure on the upkeep of which high office?
questions.explanation: Lohia drew attention in Parliament to the scale of expenditure associated with high public office, including that of the Prime Minister.

questions.q: A major hospital and an institute in Lucknow and New Delhi bear Lohia's name chiefly to honour him as a:
questions.explanation: Public institutions named after Lohia honour his role as a socialist leader and freedom fighter.
